title: Surface 1: read-only memories panel — adapter Memories/Memory/SearchMemories, GET /api/v1/memories*, web Memory tab with CLI-passthrough Edit button, apply 005-PP-POLICY classification redaction
description: Council Q2 architectural invariant (gastown-cr5 AT-DECR). Implements the read-only memories panel that was scheduled in AT-DECR Week 2 but not separately filed as a sub-bead. Depends on gastown-hu4 (THREAT_MODEL.md + 005-PP-POLICY-memories-classification-2026-05-24.md). adapter.go: Memories() / Memory(key) / SearchMemories(q) shelling to bd memories --json. handlers.go: GET /api/v1/memories, GET /api/v1/memories/{key}, GET /api/v1/memories/search?q=... — ALL read-only. Zero POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE per Q2 invariant. NEW internal/api/memoryredact.go applying 005-PP-POLICY Class A + Class B denylists with reveal toggle. web/src/api.ts + App.tsx: Memory tab read-only with Edit button that shells terminal to 'bd remember <id>' OR copies command. autocomplete=off on search box. Persistent screen-share warning banner on panel mount. v0.5.0 tag waits for this to land + gastown-3uf.

title: Foundation gates: parser.go:112 defer --until preservation fix + regression test, molecule.json → gt 0.9 wisps adapter rewrite, drop file-parsing path entirely
description: Council Q0 week-1 prerequisite (CTO binding constraint). Stop mapping deferred → pending lossily at internal/beads/parser.go:112; add IssueStatusDeferred enum + DeferredUntil time field on model.Issue. Rewrite internal/gastown/adapter.go to read wisps (gt 0.9 root-level ephemeral SQLite) instead of legacy .beads/molecule.json. Drop file-parsing path; confirm CLI-shelling-not-file-parsing invariant restored. Integration tests against real bd + gt fixture states. MUST land before any new panel work.

title: Daemon hardening + memory classification policy: 127.0.0.1 bind verified, Origin allowlist middleware, per-session token, THREAT_MODEL.md, partner-name + secret-pattern denylists
description: Council Q0 + Q2 binding constraints (CISO + GC). Verify daemon binds 127.0.0.1 not 0.0.0.0. Add Origin header allowlist middleware in internal/api. Per-session token at ~/.config/gvid/token mode 600. Author THREAT_MODEL.md in repo root. File 000-docs/005-PP-POLICY-memories-classification-2026-05-23.md with partner-name denylist (kobiton/nixtla/mudit/polygon/lit/elm) → redaction overlay; secret-pattern denylist (sk-/ghp_/AKIA/gho_/glpat-) → redacted-by-default with show-toggle that does not persist across page reloads. autocomplete=off on all input elements. Foundational for both memories panel and any future state-mutating handlers.
close_reason: Daemon hardening + memory policy landed in PR #12.

title: GoReleaser v0.5.0 release-asset upload fails with 307 redirects on intent-solutions-io repo URL
description: The Release workflow on v0.5.0 tag fired but every asset upload to https://uploads.github.com/repos/intent-solutions-io/gastown-viewer-intent/releases/328987948/assets returned 307 Temporary Redirect. GitHub's release-upload endpoint does not follow redirects, so binaries (linux_amd64, linux_arm64, darwin_*, *.deb, *.rpm, *.apk, checksums.txt) never landed on the GitHub Release. Same pattern broke v0.4.0 (run 22418230209) and v0.3.x. Root cause: the repo's canonical URL is jeremylongshore/gastown-viewer-intent but goreleaser is configured (or env hard-codes) intent-solutions-io/gastown-viewer-intent, which is a transferred alias that 307-redirects. Fix: update .goreleaser.yaml + .github/workflows/release.yaml to use jeremylongshore/ OR remove the org-name hard-code and let goreleaser auto-detect via GITHUB_REPOSITORY env. Until fixed, users who 'go install github.com/intent-solutions-io/gastown-viewer-intent/cmd/gvid@v0.5.0' get the binary via go-install (works), but Homebrew tap + direct download from release page are broken.
close_reason: GoReleaser fix landed in PR #18; v0.5.0 published.

title: Backfill web tests by 2026-07-15 — smoke-per-panel + redaction unit tests + 60% line coverage on new code
description: Closeout obligation from gastown-3uf council decision Q3 binding constraint. Add Vitest + React Testing Library to web/. One smoke test per panel: BoardColumn, IssueDetail, TownView, MoleculesList, ConvoysList, SyncPill, TriagePanel, MemoryTab (when gastown-fp0 lands). Redaction-logic unit tests on memory rendering once gastown-fp0 ships: Class A partner-name denylist coverage + Class B secret-pattern coverage per 005-PP-POLICY. 60% line coverage floor on NEW code only — pre-existing code not retroactively gated. AUTO-ESCALATES IF MISSED: council reconvenes before any further viewer work if this slips past 2026-07-15.

title: Surface 2 + 3 + closeout: dolt sync widget (header pill), human triage READ-VIEW (no POSTs this burst), tests/TESTING.md policy, backfill bead, v0.5.0 tag, alt-tab metric measurement
description: Council Q0 final-week deliverables. adapter.go: DoltStatus() / DoltRemotes() / HumanFlags() (read-only). handlers.go: GET /api/v1/sync, GET /api/v1/human (read-only). web/src/App.tsx: header sync pill (green=synced / yellow=unpushed / red=server-down) + Triage panel READ-VIEW (no respond/dismiss buttons — POSTs deferred to future burst after auth surface is tested). Tests against POST-wisps data model. tests/TESTING.md documents calibrated-investment policy explicitly (CSO SOP-consistency). File backfill-web-tests-by-2026-07-15 bead with auto-escalation. Tag v0.5.0 (not v1.0). Measure alt-tabs/day before vs after via tmux session log review (CFO outcome metric).

title: Phase 2 pre-flight: install audit-harness + markdownlint-cli2 + Vale + lychee + custom frontmatter validator; wire CI doc-quality workflow
description: Step 0 from council decision Q3 + user directive 2026-05-23. Install @intentsolutions/audit-harness as dev dep; install markdownlint-cli2 (npm), Vale (binary), lychee (binary), author custom YAML-frontmatter + Document-Filing-Standard-v4.2 filename validator. Wire .github/workflows/doc-quality.yml. Configure .vale.ini, .markdownlint-cli2.jsonc, lychee.toml. Hash-pin via pnpm exec audit-harness init. Land BEFORE any panel work begins.
close_reason: Phase 2 pre-flight tooling landed in PR #10.

title: Janitorial sweep: close PR #7, merge windows-compat follow-up commits to master, delete root-level node_modules/package.json/package-lock.json (orphan stubs from 2026-01)
description: Council Phase 2 Day 1. PR #7 stale 2mo since 2026-03-21 — review and close-or-merge. Local branch pr-8-windows-compat has 2 unpushed commits (564ca4b address-review-findings + 0a61e7a) on top of merged PR #8 — ship as a fresh PR. Root package.json is {} stub and untracked; package-lock.json untracked from Jan; delete both. Sync README + project CLAUDE.md against actual gt 0.9.0 / bd 1.0.4 supported-version state.

title: Gas Town Viewer targeted daily-value refresh — Option B council decision + execution
description: Research (3 Explore agents + PM Plan agent) diagnosed the viewer as feature-locked at v0.1.0 of bd's surface (~10% coverage) and 1-2 minor releases behind gt 0.9.0. PM recommended Option B: 3-4wk targeted refresh adding bd memories panel + dolt sync widget + human triage queue + preserve defer --until + wisps migration to replace legacy molecule.json. Phase 1: convene exec council to ratify Option B (3 open questions: cadence commitment, memories read-vs-write scope, web/TUI test debt). Phase 2 execution is contingent on council Decision Record.
notes:
GitHub: intent-solutions-io/gastown-viewer-intent#9 — https://github.com/intent-solutions-io/gastown-viewer-intent/issues/9. MIRROR RULE: bd-sync handles fan-out — `bd-sync note gastown-cr5` and `bd-sync close gastown-cr5` mirror to GH and Plane automatically.
Council session 2026-05-23 complete. AT-DECR filed at gastown-viewer-intent/000-docs/004-AT-DECR-gastown-viewer-option-b-council-2026-05-23.md. Decisions: Q0=Option B-minus (CISO novel, read-only first + daemon hardening before any mutations) with stacked CFO/CTO/GC constraints; Q1=supported-version matrix + CISO 48hr security-fast-path; Q2=read-only memories ARCHITECTURAL INVARIANT (CMO lone dissent preserved via write-capable API contract); Q3=audit-harness + bifurcated tests + smoke-per-panel + backfill bead 2026-07-15. Phase 2 execution gated on Jeremy review of AT-DECR.

title: Add Gastown integration
description:
Integrate with steveyegge/gastown multi-agent workspaces:
- Gastown adapter reading ~/gt filesystem and tmux sessions
- API endpoints for town, rigs, agents, convoys, mail
- Web UI with tabbed Beads/Gas Town view
- Agent cards, rig cards, convoy tracking

Done: commit 128878c

title: MVP demo + sanity checks
description:
Final verification of MVP:
- End-to-end test: daemon -> TUI -> Web
- Demo script for walkthrough
- Verify all acceptance criteria from PRD

Done when:
- make dev starts successfully
- TUI connects and shows board
- Web UI loads and shows board
- API health returns {status: "ok", beads_initialized: true}
- Can view issue details in both TUI and Web
- bd ready shows no blocked MVP issues

title: Dev tooling + docs in 000-docs
description:
Finalize developer experience:
- Makefile targets: dev, test, build, clean
- README with quickstart, architecture, API overview
- 000-docs/ with PRD, ADR, API spec (from templates)
- .gitignore, .editorconfig, LICENSE

Done when:
- make dev starts daemon + web (parallel)
- make test runs go test + web lint
- make build produces binaries in bin/
- README has working quickstart
- All 000-docs files complete

title: Web UI consuming API
description:
Implement web UI in web/ using Vite + React + TypeScript:
- Board view with columns
- Issue detail side panel
- Polling for updates (SSE optional for MVP)
- Tailwind or simple CSS

Done when:
- cd web && npm run dev starts on :5173
- Board shows issues grouped by status
- Click issue opens detail panel
- Shows children and dependencies
- Error state if daemon not running

title: TUI client consuming API
description:
Implement gvi-tui in cmd/gvi-tui/ using Bubbletea:
- Connects to daemon API (default http://localhost:7070)
- Board view (columns + counts)
- Issue list (filter/search)
- Issue details (children + deps)
- Keyboard navigation

Done when:
- go run ./cmd/gvi-tui starts TUI
- Board view shows issues grouped by status
- Can navigate to issue details with Enter
- Shows error if daemon not running
- q quits cleanly

title: Daemon HTTP API + SSE events
description:
Implement gvid daemon in cmd/gvid/:
- HTTP server on localhost:7070
- JSON API at /api/v1/*
- SSE endpoint at /api/v1/events
- CORS for localhost:5173 (web dev)

Endpoints:
- GET /api/v1/health - returns {status, beads_initialized, version}
- GET /api/v1/issues - list with optional filters (status, parent, search)
- GET /api/v1/issues/:id - single issue with children/deps
- GET /api/v1/board - board view grouped by status
- GET /api/v1/graph - dependency graph (JSON, optional DOT)
- GET /api/v1/events - SSE stream

Done when:
- go run ./cmd/gvid starts server on :7070
- curl http://localhost:7070/api/v1/health returns JSON
- All endpoints return valid JSON or SSE
- Returns 503 with actionable error if beads not initialized

title: Beads adapter via bd CLI
description:
Implement internal/beads.Adapter interface that shells out to bd CLI:
- ListIssues(ctx, filters) []Issue
- GetIssue(ctx, id) Issue
- Board(ctx) Board
- Graph(ctx) Graph

Implementation:
- Execute bd commands (bd list, bd show, bd board, bd blocked)
- Parse output with graceful degradation
- Return partial data + warnings on parse errors
- Never panic on unexpected output

Done when:
- internal/beads/adapter.go defines Adapter interface
- internal/beads/cli.go implements CLI executor
- internal/beads/parser.go handles output parsing
- Unit tests with mock bd output pass
- Returns clear error if bd not found

title: Domain model + event schema
description:
Define core domain types in internal/model/:
- Issue struct (id, title, status, priority, parent, children, blocks, blocked_by, timestamps)
- Board struct (columns by status, issue summaries)
- Graph struct (nodes, edges with dependency types)
- Event types for SSE (issue_created, issue_updated, heartbeat)

Done when:
- internal/model/issue.go exists with Issue struct
- internal/model/board.go exists with Board + Column structs
- internal/model/graph.go exists with Graph, Node, Edge structs
- internal/model/event.go exists with Event types
- go build ./internal/model/... succeeds

title: Gastown Viewer Intent MVP
description:
Local-first Mission Control for Beads + Gastown-style agent swarms.

MVP delivers:
- Go daemon with HTTP JSON API on localhost:7070
- TUI client (Bubbletea) consuming API
- Web UI (Vite + React + TypeScript) consuming API
- Board view + issue details + graph export
- Events stream (SSE preferred; polling acceptable)

Done when:
- make dev starts daemon + web
- TUI shows board and opens issue details
- API returns valid JSON for all endpoints
- Graceful error when Beads not initialized

title: HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN: rotate secret + re-enable brews block in .goreleaser.yaml
description: After fixing the 307-redirect bug in gastown-vjt, the v0.5.0 release uploaded all 11 binary assets successfully to the GitHub Releases page. However the brew-tap push step then failed with 401 Bad credentials on intent-solutions-io/homebrew-tap (the tap repo IS at that org per gh api). Token in HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN GitHub secret either expired, lacks write scope, or was for a different account. To re-enable the homebrew tap path: rotate HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN in the repo's Settings → Secrets → Actions, then uncomment the brews block in .goreleaser.yaml + re-run release on the next tag. Workaround for users in the meantime: 'go install github.com/jeremylongshore/gastown-viewer-intent/cmd/gvid@latest' OR direct download from https://github.com/jeremylongshore/gastown-viewer-intent/releases/tag/v0.5.0 — both work.
